top_signals.title: Stocks Rise and Bond Yields Fall After CPI Data: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Stocks rose and bond yields fell after softer-than-estimated inflation data tempered concern about a resurgence in oil prices that could prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020, Core Gauge Unchanged
top_signals.summary: US consumer prices declined in June for the first time in six years and a key gauge of underlying inflation was little changed, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: U.S. June CPI fell 0.4%, likely cooling move toward Fed rate hikes
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Warsh Says Fed Has ‘No Tolerance’ for Elevated Inflation
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said policymakers at the central bank have no tolerance for high inflation, reiterating a vow to tame price growth that has been elevated for five years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
top_signals.summary: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
